candidates.why: Primary operating top-line revenue; matches definition of total revenue/sales and appears as the main operating revenue line.
Explicit total revenue line with identical amounts to Operating revenue; included as secondary option in case label changes over time.

variables.notes: Both 'Operating revenue' and 'Total Revenue' represent the same top-line; providing both as alternative labels over time.

candidates.why: Generic operating expenses; could mix overhead and other items, less clearly direct COGS.
Aggregate operating expenses including multiple components; likely includes SG&A and depreciation, not pure COGS.
Broad operating expense category; appears more like SG&A/overhead than direct COGS.
'Cost of Goods' explicitly denotes cost of goods sold / direct production costs, matching COGS definition even if recent columns show nan.
Non-operating result, not COGS-related.
Staff/overhead, more SG&A-like than direct COGS and also appears unused here.
Balance sheet liability, not an income statement cost flow like COGS.

variables.notes: Selected the explicit 'Cost of Goods' line as COGS; other candidate expense rows are broader operating/overhead categories.

candidates.why: Personnel costs that are classic SG&A overhead; clearly not labeled as COGS and not a total line.
Likely small or residual operating overhead category; treated as SG&A component despite nan preview, per instructions that presence implies values in some year.

Aggregate operating expenses line that likely includes components like wages, other operating expenses, depreciation, and impairments; avoided because component rows exist and totals should be excluded for SG&A mapping.
Broad operating overhead bucket distinct from depreciation/impairment; fits SG&A other operating expenses.

candidates.row_label: Managment Services and Other General Exp
candidates.why: Explicit general expenses/management services, clearly SG&A-type overhead even though preview is nan.

final_choice.row_label: Managment Services and Other General Exp

variables.notes: Chose multiple SG&A components (personnel and other operating/general expenses) and excluded the aggregate 'Operating expenses' total consistent with the rule to prefer components over totals and to avoid totals for XSGA.

No explicit research & development line (e.g., R&D, research, development) appears in the income statement preview; 'Exploration Cost' is industry-specific and not standard R&D, so XRD is treated as absent.

candidates.why: Core interest expense line with negative values; good representation of interest expense in several years.
Additional interest expense component; should be a lower-priority alternative label for interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest expense specifically on lease liabilities; clearly interest cost and a relevant component if main interest line is missing for some years.
Represents income attribution to minorities, not an interest expense; should not be used for XINT.
Generic interest expense label (though preview nan); semantically the best match for XINT and should be top priority when populated.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease interest; can serve as backup if primary interest lines are unavailable.
Equity attributable to majority owners, not interest expense.
Equity attributable to NCI, not interest expense.
Book value of minority equity, not interest expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Provided a priority list of income-statement interest expense labels, with the generic 'Interest Expense' preferred, followed by other specific interest cost lines. Equity-related ‘interest’ labels were excluded.

candidates.why: Component of equity but not the full equity attributable to owners; using it alone would understate book equity.
Represents total equity including non-controlling interests; acceptable only if no separate parent-equity line exists, but here a better parent-equity proxy appears to be 'Majority interest'.
Technical balancing line, not an economic equity measure for PROF.
Represents total assets/liabilities, not equity alone.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. The best conceptual BE measure is the parent/owner equity excluding NCI, which appears to be labeled 'Majority interest' on the balance sheet even though it is not in the candidate list. Using 'Total equity' would double-count with MIB, so 'Majority interest' is chosen from the full A-column and flagged for manual review to confirm it indeed represents equity attributable to owners of the parent. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Balance Sheet] Majority interest

candidates.why: Income statement allocation of profit to minorities, not the balance sheet minority equity amount needed for MIB.
Income statement minority interest share of profit, not the book value of minority equity.
Balance sheet line for equity attributable to non-controlling interests; primary choice for MIB.
Alternative label for non-controlling interests with identical amounts; good backup if primary label is missing in some years.

variables.notes: Mapped MIB to the balance sheet minority equity lines so BE (Majority interest) and MIB sum to Total equity without double counting.
